## [00:00 - 03:00]

In this lesson, you'll learn how to protect your brand when you're dealing with overseas manufacturers. This is extremely important because your product is your IP and you want to protect your IP. You don't want your IP to be stolen by either your manufacturers or the other clients at the manufacturer. This is very common. A lot of people's designs get stolen at the manufacturer. This happened to me many, many times because we designed and developed furniture items before and we gave the design to a manufacturer. One day, I saw the same design, even the same pictures got used on a different site at selling. I immediately called the manufacturer and basically it was one of the workers who brought the design to a different factory and started producing and selling on a local website. I immediately asked them to remove all the listings. They did, which was really, really good. The second instance was actually there was one factory worker pre-registered by brand name in China. This also happened to me once. He was also good enough to return the brand name back to me. That was really, really good. There was also another time, which was very, very funny because we were at the factory talking about a US startup going super, super fast and the brand is called this, this and that. A few months after, I noticed that he registered this trademark in China successfully as well. There are several steps you can take. The first one is definitely to sign a mutual NDA with your manufacturer. Letting them know the importance of your IP and the protection of it and get them acknowledged that. While sometimes it doesn't mean too much from the legal side of things, it's still basically getting them to commit to protect your IP. The second thing is to issue them a brand authorization letter because you don't want them to start producing things under your name without pre-agreed by you. Usually I give them a brand authorization letter with one year time limit on it. By the end of the one year, I'll either renew or terminate the agreement. In order to do this, I've actually included the template for an NDA and the brand authorization letter in the resource section that you can download and repurpose for your need.

## [03:00 - 03:12]

The action item for this lesson is to download the templates I included in this resource, so the NDA and brand authorization letter and rewrite it, send it to your supplier and get them to sign.
